raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Disadvantaged and low-income communities have less canopy cover than other communities due to the cost of tree planting and tree care.",
    "These communities struggle to fund and maintain urban forest programs, leading to an inequitable distribution of environmental benefits.",
    "Urban heat island effects and worse air pollution are more prevalent in tree-poor areas.",
    "Senator Caballero's SB 347, The Neighborhood Tree Fund, provides a consistent source of funding to CalFire’s Urban and Community Forestry program.",
    "Mature tree canopies in wealthy neighborhoods provide shade, reduce heat, and improve the physical and mental health of the community.",
    "The nearly 300 trees planted in the Huron area will help to cool the streets and create walkable neighborhoods.",
    "The planted trees will sequester carbon, provide oxygen, and help to improve health."
  ]
}
```
